notes.anchor: A curse on Segulius from the gods
notes.note: Segulius (Labeo Segulius) is the rumour-monger of the preceding letter (Fam. 11.20); Cicero answers Decimus Brutus's report of his gossip with this mock-imprecation.

The commission of ten (decemviri) for assigning land and rewards to soldiers; Cicero says he so disliked serving on it that he wishes he were not a member, and that he had Octavian and Decimus Brutus deliberately excepted from it.
Decimus Brutus was consul-designate for 42 BC; Cicero invokes the authority of that coming consulship as a guarantee of security.

The agraria curatio, the magistracy charged with allotting land to veterans; Cicero says he blocked rival senators who were angling to control it and kept the matter reserved for himself and Decimus Brutus.

notes.anchor: The day before the Nones of June.
notes.note: By inclusive Roman counting, the day before the Nones (the 5th) of June, i.e. 4 June 43 BC; the letter was written at Rome.
